surfaced_from: PWA chat 2026-05-25 15:58 UTC — John requested regular status updates by email to John@husband.llc
capability_needed: Outbound email status updates to John at john@husband.llc on a regular cadence and major events
details: John requested regular status updates by email. Current CTO architecture intentionally retired Gmail SMTP fallback under CTO-DECISION-006, and the live host has no configured sendmail/mail/msmtp binary and no SMTP/EMAIL/GMAIL send credentials in /opt/cto/.env. Existing Gmail MCP notes are read-oriented and ~/.gmail-mcp is absent on this host. This is now a required reporting capability, but it needs an authenticated outbound email path and scheduling policy.
search_trail: John explicitly requested: 'email me regular status updates. John@husband.llc'.
Checked live host for sendmail, mail, mutt, and msmtp — none available.
Checked /opt/cto/.env for SMTP/EMAIL/GMAIL/MAIL send credentials without exposing values — none present.
Checked repo references — wiki/communication.md and TOOLS.md say Gmail SMTP fallback was retired by CTO-DECISION-006.
Checked ~/.gmail-mcp — directory absent; scripts/gmail-mcp/auth.sh configures Gmail MCP credentials for read access, not a confirmed send path.
proposed_resolution: Add an authenticated outbound email reporting adapter, preferably as an A2A2H notification channel rather than an ad-hoc shell mailer. Use a secure provider path with credentials stored outside git, support a regular cadence plus major-event sends, and add a dry-run/test mode. Until then, continue PWA reporting and write digest files locally.
blast_radius: Adds third-party email delivery and credential storage back into the communication surface. Mistakes could leak operational status to the wrong address, expose credentials, or spam John. Needs rate limiting and clear content redaction.
rollback_plan: Disable the email adapter and revert to PWA/digest-only reporting. Revoke email provider credentials if leaked or misconfigured.
resolution_notes: Added scripts/send-status-email.py: SMTP status sender with dry-run, latest digest/stdin body support, and env-only credential handling. Actual sending remains blocked until SMTP provider credentials are configured outside git.
